commit.message:
Add progress feedback for large collection fetching (#21)

Closes #20 
Large collections (hundreds of items) produce no output while resolving
external IDs per-item via HTTP, making the tool appear hung.

### Changes

- **`plex.Client.OnProgress` callback** — called with `(current, total)`
after each item is processed in `GetCollectionItems`, where the per-item
metadata lookups are the bottleneck
- **High-level status in `resolveCollection`** — prints
finding/fetching/loaded messages to stdout so the user sees activity
immediately
- **Stderr progress line in `main()`** — `newCollectionProgressFunc`
writes `\rProcessing collection items: N/M` with in-place
carriage-return updates; newline on completion

```
Finding collection "Movies"...
Fetching items for "Movies"...
Processing collection items: 142/200   ← stderr, in-place
Loaded 200 items from "Movies"
```

The callback is nil-safe — no behavior change when unset (e.g., in tests
or the bridge package).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nipuna-perera <11575062+nipuna-perera@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Nipuna perera <nipuna.royal@gmail.com>

Publish v2: add mariadb importer (#11)

* feat: add SecForm4MariadbInserter node and MySqlCredentials

Add a new n8n node that takes parsed SEC Form 4 data and upserts it
into a MySQL/MariaDB database. Features:

- MySqlCredentials credential type for database connection
- Idempotent upserts using INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
- Automatic table creation (10 normalized tables) via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
- Transactional writes with rollback on error
- Configurable table prefix (default: sec_)
- 18-test suite covering SQL generation, transactions, and rollback

commit.message:
Update laststanding: Linux TLS via BearSSL amalgamation

Update submodule to pick up Linux TLS support (BearSSL). Embed
bearssl_amalg.c (~991KB) alongside l_tls.h so freestanding builds
on Linux get TLS automatically. No Oscan code changes needed —
the existing l_tls_* wrappers now call real implementations on Linux
instead of stubs returning -1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Deep testing: 37 new tests, 4 bug fixes

Bugs fixed (found by GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.3-Codex stress testing):

- MatterStore.list(limit=0) returned all entities instead of none;
  now correctly honors zero and clamps negative limits
- compute_deadline parsed YYYY-MM-DD dates in UTC, causing off-by-one
  errors on holiday/weekend boundaries; fixed with local date parsing
- generate_invoice accepted zero/negative hours without warning;
  now skips invalid entries and returns warnings array
- process_document reported lineCount=1 for empty content; fixed to 0

New test files:
- tests/agent/stress-scenarios.test.ts (29 tests): MatterStore
  isolation across 100 entities and 5 matters, unicode/special chars,
  entity update semantics, deletion + link cleanup, search relevance,
  linking chains and edge cases, tool execution errors, system prompt
  verification, conflict checker, chronology engine edge cases
- tests/agent/product-quality.test.ts (8 tests): full litigation
  lifecycle, concurrent operations, privilege marker handling, real
  FRCP deadline scenarios, document processing pipeline, invoice
  arithmetic accuracy, cross-module linking, search relevance

521 tests total, all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Caddy checksum verification: use SHA-512 to match upstream checksums

Caddy's checksums.txt uses SHA-512 (128 hex chars), but the verification
code expected SHA-256 (64 hex chars). This caused caddyChecksumFromFile
to skip valid hash entries and fail with 'failed to find sha256 checksum'.

Changes:
- Switch from crypto/sha256 to crypto/sha512
- Update hash length check from 64 to 128
- Rename sha256File to sha512File
- Update test to verify SHA-512 behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(bot): extract BFS crawler with Fetcher abstraction

Decouple BFS traversal logic from HTTP fetching to enable unit testing.

**Changes:**
- Add crawler package with Fetcher interface
  - FileFetcher for file-based testing (no network required)
  - HTTPFetcher for production HTTP requests
- Implement complete BFS with FIFO queue
- Add URL utilities (normalize, domain validation, extension filtering)
- Add HTML link extraction with golang.org/x/net/html
- Create 17 unit tests with 84.2% coverage
- Update bot/README.md with crawler usage examples

**Architecture:**
- Dependency inversion: BFSCrawler depends on Fetcher interface
- Strategy pattern: swap FileFetcher/HTTPFetcher without changing crawler
- Context-aware: all operations support cancellation

**Testing:**
- All tests pass using FileFetcher with HTML fixtures
- No network required for testing
- Tests verify: depth limits, domain filtering, BFS order, deduplication

**OpenSpec:**
- Modified bot-pioneer-crawler spec (added Fetcher scenarios)
- Archived change: openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-12-simple-bfs-crawler
- Pioneer integration deferred (4 tasks remaining)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolve dependent alias member template args

* Normalize class template cache keys with defaults

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/15d35c97-82d4-4e41-b1a0-3c90e96d6443

* Alias raw defaulted template instantiations

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/15d35c97-82d4-4e41-b1a0-3c90e96d6443

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip redundant normalized cache lookup when raw key was already checked

When can_use_raw_cache_key is true, the normalized key is identical to
the raw key that was already checked earlier. Wrap the normalized cache
lookup in `if (!can_use_raw_cache_key)` to avoid a redundant hash-map
lookup on the common path where all args are explicitly provided.

* Add test for dependent alias and defaulted template instantiation fixes

* Fix defaulted nested alias lookup

Add countdown widget

  configurable start time, adds seconds on sub/donation events

Fix MatterStore link resolution bug, add functional scenario tests

Bug fix:
- getLinkedEntities() used raw entity ID instead of matter-namespaced
  composite key when looking up linked entities, causing all link
  lookups to silently return empty results

Tests (23 new tests across 2 files):
- tests/agent/functional-scenarios.test.ts: 8 scenarios covering
  matter intake, document review/tagging, deposition prep,
  cross-module search, matter isolation (security), deadline
  computation, invoice generation, and entity linking validation
- tests/web/frontend-scenarios.test.ts: 15 tests covering mock API
  chat responses, document content, data completeness, type
  consistency, and component wiring

All 484 tests pass.

commit.message:
ci: add PR checks workflow, pom.xml scaffold, and pipeline hardening

ci-cd.yml:
- Add concurrency group to cancel outdated runs on main
- Add Postgres 16 service container to build-test job
- Pass SPRING_DATASOURCE_* env vars to mvn verify
- Upload JaCoCo HTML report as artifact
- Add health-check polling after Render deploy hook (24 retries x 10s = 4min timeout)

New ci.yml (PR Checks):
- Triggers on PR to main and develop
- Job 1: build-test with Postgres + JaCoCo coverage enforcement
- Job 2: static-analysis with SpotBugs (findsecbugs) + OWASP dependency-check
- Concurrency group per PR number

pom.xml:
- Spring Boot 3.3.0 parent, Java 21
- Core deps: web, data-jpa, security, actuator, validation, postgresql, flyway
- MapStruct 1.5.5.Final + Lombok
- JaCoCo 0.8.12 with 70% LINE coverage threshold on BUNDLE
- SpotBugs 4.8.4.0 + findsecbugs 1.13.0 (effort Max)
- OWASP dependency-check 10.0.3 (fail on CVSS >= 7)

New required secret: RENDER_APP_URL (public Render service URL)
New optional secret: NVD_API_KEY (for faster OWASP NVD database sync)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
003 esp dsp equalization (#5)

* feat: add parametric equalizer via esp-dsp biquad chain

- Add espressif/esp-dsp dependency (idf_component.yml, CMakeLists.txt)
- Add EQFilterType enum, EQBandConfig struct, EQConfig to DeviceConfig (SCHEMA_VERSION=2)
- Implement EQProcessor: DRAM_ATTR static state, Audio EQ Cookbook peaking
  formula, dsps_biquad_sf32 stereo interleaved chain, lock-free coef updates
- Integrate EQProcessor into DMA audio loop (audio_capture.cpp)
- Persist 10-band EQ config via NVS blob with factory defaults
- Add REST API: GET/POST /api/eq, POST /api/eq/reset, GET /settings/eq
- Add EQ settings page to web UI (config_portal_html.h nav link)
- Mark all 29 tasks complete in specs/003-esp-dsp-equalization/tasks.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add missing cstring include and restore HTTPServer::init signature

- config_schema.h: add #include <cstring> for strcmp in eq_filter_type_from_str
- http_server.cpp: restore missing 'bool HTTPServer::init(uint16_t, uint32_t)' signature
  (was accidentally dropped when inserting EQ settings page handler)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Updated deps

* fix: increase main task stack size 3584→8192 for EQ DeviceConfig growth

DeviceConfig grew by ~164 bytes after adding eq_bands[10] + eq_enabled.
Multiple DeviceConfig locals (main.cpp:97, mqtt_client.cpp:79/531) stacked
at call depth app_main→start_wifi_connected→mqtt_init caused overflow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add explicit @Column(name) for boolean fields to fix is_combo DB error; add renewalDayUserSet + per-user renewalDay

- Subscription: add @Column(name='is_combo') to fix 'column does not exist' error;
  add @Column(name='renewal_day_user_set') renewalDayUserSet flag — admin marks
  that each user sets their own renewal day
- UserSubscriptionEntry: add @Column(name='active'), add renewalDay field
  for user-set renewal day (used when sub.renewalDayUserSet=true)
- UserStore.addSubscription: reads renewalDay from request body
- AdminPage: SubscriptionInlineForm shows 'Ustawi użytkownik' checkbox on
  renewalDay — when checked hides number input; CompanyDetailView shows
  '👤 ustawi użytkownik' badge
- CompanyPage: subscribe modal shows renewalDay input when sub.renewalDayUserSet=true
- AccountPage: nextRenewal prefers entry.renewalDay over sub.renewalDay

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
